sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content:
Primary curative treatment for all thyroid carcinoma types. Also used for recurrent or persistent disease.

resectability_criteria: For anaplastic carcinoma, resectability depends on extent of involved structures, potential morbidity, and mortality. R0/R1 resection is goal. In most cases, debulking surgery is not indicated. For differentiated thyroid carcinomas, completeness of resection is assessed by margins and invasion. [ANAP-2, ST-1]

pre_operative_optimisation: Complete workup including imaging, molecular testing, and multidisciplinary consultation. For medullary carcinoma, pheochromocytoma must be excluded before surgery. For anaplastic carcinoma, expedient evaluation and consideration of neoadjuvant therapy if borderline resectable. [MEDU-1, ANAP-1, ANAP-2]

cards.name: Procedure
cards.detail: {"name": "Total Thyroidectomy", "approach": "Surgical removal of the entire thyroid gland with or without central neck dissection.", "indications": "Papillary carcinoma with T3/T4, N1, M1, aggressive histology, prior radiation, bilateral nodularity, or tumor >4 cm. Medullary carcinoma (all cases). Anaplastic carcinoma if resectable. Follicular/oncocytic carcinoma with invasive features (≥4 vessels, gross ETE). [PAP-1, FOLL-1, ONC-1, MEDU-1, ANAP-2]", "key_complications": "Hypoparathyroidism (transient 5.4%, permanent 1.1%), recurrent laryngeal nerve injury (transient 3.4%, permanent 3.4%), superior laryngeal nerve injury. [228, 229, 230]", "operative_mortality": "Low; not specified in source but generally <1%."}

cards.name: Procedure
cards.detail: {"name": "Lobectomy/Isthmusectomy", "approach": "Removal of one thyroid lobe and isthmus.", "indications": "Low-risk papillary carcinoma ≤4 cm without high-risk features. Follicular/oncocytic neoplasm without evidence of invasion. Preferred for patients who cannot tolerate bilateral surgery or refuse lifelong thyroid hormone. [PAP-1, FOLL-1, ONC-1]", "key_complications": "Similar to total thyroidectomy but unilateral hypoparathyroidism possible. Recurrent laryngeal nerve injury risk lower.", "operative_mortality": "Very low."}

cards.name: Procedure
cards.detail: {"name": "Therapeutic Neck Dissection", "approach": "Formal compartmental resection of involved nodal basins (central level VI, lateral levels II–V).", "indications": "Clinically apparent or biopsy-proven nodal metastases. Not recommended as prophylaxis for papillary carcinoma with cN0. [PAP-1, MEDU-1]", "key_complications": "Injury to spinal accessory nerve, phrenic nerve, or jugular vein. Chylorrhea.", "operative_mortality": "Low."}

cards.name: Procedure
cards.detail: {"name": "Prophylactic Thyroidectomy", "approach": "Total thyroidectomy in children/infants with RET PV mutations.", "indications": "Hereditary medullary carcinoma (MEN2A/MEN2B) based on RET mutation risk. Timing varies: highest-risk (M918T) during first year of life; high-risk (codon 634, A883F) before age 5. [MEDU-3, MEDU-4]", "key_complications": "Same as total thyroidectomy; higher risk in very young children.", "operative_mortality": "Very low in experienced hands."}
